pages.focus: Residual alpha among AI-adjacent passives, power, grid, connectors, ODM/rack, cooling, memory/storage and physical-infrastructure bottleneck suppliers.
Comparison of human/carbon consumption proxies with silicon/AI-cluster consumption proxies.

methodology.summary: Each stock in the two existing universes was evaluated one at a time for current alpha-ranking relevance as of 2026-06-08. The assessment starts from the source rank and company fields, then overlays fresh web research on AI compute demand, data-center electricity and grid bottlenecks, current company evidence, market-cap scale, YTD rerating and likely passive/index crowding.
methodology.marketDataNote: Latest price, market capitalization and YTD-return inputs were taken from the universe CSV snapshots whose market_data_as_of fields are 2026-06-08; the 2026-06-08 overlay emphasizes current source/news evidence and rank relevance rather than recalculating prices independently.

rankMoveDefinition.up: Would promote if the universe were refreshed because hidden/under-owned upside and direct bottleneck evidence outweigh valuation/crowding.
rankMoveDefinition.hold: Current rank remains directionally appropriate after the current-news and valuation/crowding overlay.
rankMoveDefinition.down: Still relevant, but should be demoted because rerating, market cap, passive ownership/index crowding or indirect exposure reduces alpha.
rankMoveDefinition.remove: Evidence no longer supports inclusion in the alpha universe.
rankMoveDefinition.add: New candidate not in the source universe; no add rows were inserted because this pass was scoped to the two current universes.

factors: Directness to AI 2027 compute demand and AI-factory buildout
Under-owned/hidden nature adjusted for market capitalization and passive/index ownership likelihood
YTD run-up as a proxy for how much alpha has already been discovered
Passive components: MLCCs, inductors, shunts, current sensing and power conversion
Power, grid, switchgear, transformers, cabling, onsite power and cooling bottlenecks
Connectors, optics, networking, packaging, PCB/substrates, HBM and storage constraints
Human-vs-silicon consumption thesis relevance for carbon-vs-silicon names
Primary/current company evidence where available, supplemented by current industry/government sources
limitations: This is a research-ranking artifact, not investment advice.
Some non-US issuers have less current English-language primary evidence; confidence is lower where evidence is indirect or dated.
Market-cap and YTD inputs are carried from 2026-06-03 CSVs because live market data was not the requested output field.

rows.bullCase: 2026-06-08: Sumida has upside if higher AI rack power density pulls more MLCCs, inductors, shunts and current-sense parts into PSU, IBC and VRM designs. Its Inductors exposure (Magnetics and inductors for power conversion) is most alpha-relevant when investors still treat it as a supplier detail rather than obvious AI beta.
rows.neutralCase: 2026-06-08: A hold case keeps the stock near its current rank because the universe already captures very under-owned, ~$0.33B micro/small-cap scale, and moderate 34.0% YTD move against the strength of current AI-infrastructure demand.
rows.bearCase: 2026-06-08: The bear case is that liquidity, customer concentration and small-cap cyclicality can dominate even when the bottleneck thesis is right. In that scenario, Sumida loses rank relevance even if the AI 2027 demand thesis remains intact.
rows.rankMoveRationale: Hold: rank 1 still balances direct bottleneck relevance against valuation, ~$0.33B micro/small-cap scale, moderate 34.0% YTD move, passive crowding and execution risk.
